nodes.name: fm.ca.cert.missing.at.join
nodes.summary:
If /var/lib/globular/pki/ca.key or ca.crt is absent when a node attempts to join,
the CSR signing step fails. Depending on error handling, the join may return a
generic error or silently issue an unsigned cert. The joining node installs a bad
cert and all subsequent mTLS connections fail. The root cause is usually a failed
prior bootstrap that left PKI in a partial state.

nodes.name: xds.cert.validated.before.serving.secrets
nodes.summary:
When xDS builds the TLS secret response for Envoy SDS, it must verify that the
cert it is about to serve is signed by the cluster CA and has not expired.
Serving an expired or self-signed cert causes Envoy to reject connections
cluster-wide. A missing file must return an error, not serve an empty secret.

nodes.name: metric_warning:workflow_failed_runs_15m
nodes.summary: Workflow failures indicate convergence risk and may threaten reconcile fairness

metadata.explanation: Workflow failures indicate convergence risk and may threaten reconcile fairness

nodes.name: metric_warning:scylla_disk_percent
nodes.summary: ScyllaDB disk pressure risks workflow/AI memory backend availability

metadata.explanation: ScyllaDB disk pressure risks workflow/AI memory backend availability

nodes.name: xds.mtls.required.in.production
nodes.summary:
The xDS server serves TLS secrets to Envoy. In production, the connection between
xDS and Envoy must be mutually authenticated with the cluster CA.
GLOBULAR_XDS_INSECURE=1 disables this check and must never be set in production.
An AI agent must not add logic that defaults to insecure when the env var is absent
or treats its absence as an error.

nodes.name: join.phase.order.enforced
nodes.summary:
The 8-phase join flow has strict dependencies: each phase builds on the state
established by the prior phase. Phase 5 (etcd member add) requires Phase 4
(binary sync). Phase 6 (service start) requires Phase 5 (etcd member added).
An AI agent must not add a shortcut that skips phases, and must not reorder
phases to "optimize" the flow.

nodes.name: pki.ca.canonical.path
nodes.summary:
The CA key, CA cert, and all issued service certs live under /var/lib/globular/pki/.
No code may construct a PKI path relative to the domain name, a config variable,
or the current working directory. Paths like /etc/globular/creds/ do not exist.
Constructing a wrong path silently falls back to an unsigned or missing cert,
causing mTLS failures that are hard to diagnose.

nodes.name: fm.join.token.expired.mid.phase
nodes.summary:
A slow join (large binary download, slow network) may take long enough for the
join token to expire. If expiry is only checked at Phase 1 and not re-validated
at Phase 5, the gateway proceeds with an invalid token, signs a CSR for an
unauthenticated node, adds an etcd member, and then fails authentication checks
later — leaving a ghost etcd member and a mis-issued cert.

nodes.name: csr.signature.verified.before.signing
nodes.summary:
The join flow accepts a CSR from the joining node. Before the gateway CA signs the
CSR, it must verify: (1) the join token is valid and not expired, (2) the CSR
subject matches the expected node identity, (3) the CSR signature is valid
(the node holds the private key). Signing without these checks issues a cert to
any caller who submits a CSR to the join endpoint.

nodes.name: fm.xds.insecure.in.production
nodes.summary:
If GLOBULAR_XDS_INSECURE=1 is set in a production environment, the xDS server
disables mutual TLS. Any process that can reach the xDS port can push arbitrary
Envoy configuration — routing all traffic to an attacker-controlled backend,
replacing TLS secrets, or disabling auth entirely. This is a full cluster
compromise vector.

nodes.name: repair.etcd.explicit.flag.only
nodes.summary:
When a node's prior join left a stale etcd WAL, re-running join will fail because
etcd refuses to start with an existing data directory that doesn't match the
cluster. The --repair-etcd flag signals the join handler to wipe the WAL before
starting etcd. This must never happen implicitly — a silent WAL wipe destroys
any local etcd data and must require explicit operator intent.

nodes.name: fm.csr.signed.without.validation
nodes.summary:
If the CSR signing handler signs any CSR submitted to the join endpoint without
first validating the join token, the CSR subject matching the expected node
identity, and the CSR signature (proving key ownership), then any caller who
reaches the join endpoint can obtain a valid cluster CA-signed cert. This cert
can be used to pass mTLS checks for any cluster service.

nodes.name: gateway.no.etcd.writes
nodes.summary:
The gateway is the cluster entry point — it signs CSRs, proxies requests, and
serves the join flow. It must NOT write service configuration, desired state,
node records, or any cluster policy to etcd. All etcd writes for cluster state
go through the controller. If the gateway writes to etcd, it breaks the
single-authority model and creates split-brain state.

nodes.name: metric_warning:minio_offline_disks
nodes.summary: Offline MinIO disks risk artifact availability and violate topology contract

metadata.explanation: Offline MinIO disks risk artifact availability and violate topology contract

nodes.name: fm.etcd.ghost.member
nodes.summary:
If a node starts a join, reaches Phase 5 (etcd member add), but then fails before
completing Phase 6 (etcd start), the new member is registered in the etcd member
list but never comes online. Subsequent join attempts for the same node fail because
etcd rejects adding a member with the same peer URL or sees quorum already
reduced by the non-responsive ghost. The ghost must be explicitly removed.

nodes.name: etcd.ghost.cleared.before.member.add
nodes.summary:
If a node was partially joined in a prior attempt, it may have a ghost entry in
the etcd member list. Adding a new member for the same node without first removing
the ghost causes etcd quorum failures. The join handler must detect and remove
stale member entries for the joining node's identity before calling member add.

nodes.name: join.token.validated.before.phase
nodes.summary:
The 8-phase join flow must validate the join token at Phase 1 entry. If the join
is long-running (slow network, large binary sync), the token expiry must be
re-checked before Phase 5 (etcd member add), which is the point of no return.
Proceeding with an expired token past Phase 5 leaves a ghost etcd member.

nodes.name: metric_warning:etcd_fsync_latency_ms
nodes.summary: High etcd fsync latency risks leader election instability and workflow dispatch timeouts

metadata.explanation: High etcd fsync latency risks leader election instability and workflow dispatch timeouts

nodes.name: metric_warning:etcd_disk_percent
nodes.summary: etcd disk full triggers NOSPACE alarm, blocks all writes, halts convergence

metadata.explanation: etcd disk full triggers NOSPACE alarm, blocks all writes, halts convergence

nodes.name: fm.xds.snapshot.stale
nodes.summary:
When a TLS cert is renewed or an Envoy cluster/listener config changes, the xDS
server must push a new snapshot. If the push is missed (gateway restart, xDS crash,
or race between cert renewal and snapshot rebuild), Envoy continues serving with the
old config. The service appears healthy but is using an expired cert or routing to
a stale set of backends. Envoy does not self-detect snapshot staleness.

invariants.title: Join token must be validated before Phase 1; re-checked before Phase 5
invariants.summary:
The 8-phase join flow must validate the join token at Phase 1 entry. If the join
is long-running (slow network, large binary sync), the token expiry must be
re-checked before Phase 5 (etcd member add), which is the point of no return.
Proceeding with an expired token past Phase 5 leaves a ghost etcd member.

invariants.title: xDS must verify cert chain before serving TLS secrets to Envoy
invariants.summary:
When xDS builds the TLS secret response for Envoy SDS, it must verify that the
cert it is about to serve is signed by the cluster CA and has not expired.
Serving an expired or self-signed cert causes Envoy to reject connections
cluster-wide. A missing file must return an error, not serve an empty secret.

invariants.title: CSR must be verified (node identity + token) before the CA signs it
invariants.summary:
The join flow accepts a CSR from the joining node. Before the gateway CA signs the
CSR, it must verify: (1) the join token is valid and not expired, (2) the CSR
subject matches the expected node identity, (3) the CSR signature is valid
(the node holds the private key). Signing without these checks issues a cert to
any caller who submits a CSR to the join endpoint.

invariants.title: PKI files must be read from /var/lib/globular/pki/ — never relative to domain or cwd
invariants.summary:
The CA key, CA cert, and all issued service certs live under /var/lib/globular/pki/.
No code may construct a PKI path relative to the domain name, a config variable,
or the current working directory. Paths like /etc/globular/creds/ do not exist.
Constructing a wrong path silently falls back to an unsigned or missing cert,
causing mTLS failures that are hard to diagnose.

invariants.title: xDS must use mTLS in production; GLOBULAR_XDS_INSECURE is dev-only
invariants.summary:
The xDS server serves TLS secrets to Envoy. In production, the connection between
xDS and Envoy must be mutually authenticated with the cluster CA.
GLOBULAR_XDS_INSECURE=1 disables this check and must never be set in production.
An AI agent must not add logic that defaults to insecure when the env var is absent
or treats its absence as an error.

invariants.title: Ghost etcd members must be removed before adding the new member in Phase 5
invariants.summary:
If a node was partially joined in a prior attempt, it may have a ghost entry in
the etcd member list. Adding a new member for the same node without first removing
the ghost causes etcd quorum failures. The join handler must detect and remove
stale member entries for the joining node's identity before calling member add.

invariants.title: etcd WAL wipe requires --repair-etcd; never implicit
invariants.summary:
When a node's prior join left a stale etcd WAL, re-running join will fail because
etcd refuses to start with an existing data directory that doesn't match the
cluster. The --repair-etcd flag signals the join handler to wipe the WAL before
starting etcd. This must never happen implicitly — a silent WAL wipe destroys
any local etcd data and must require explicit operator intent.

invariants.title: Gateway must not write desired state to etcd; only controller may do this
invariants.summary:
The gateway is the cluster entry point — it signs CSRs, proxies requests, and
serves the join flow. It must NOT write service configuration, desired state,
node records, or any cluster policy to etcd. All etcd writes for cluster state
go through the controller. If the gateway writes to etcd, it breaks the
single-authority model and creates split-brain state.

invariants.title: Join phases 1-8 must execute in order; no phase may be skipped
invariants.summary:
The 8-phase join flow has strict dependencies: each phase builds on the state
established by the prior phase. Phase 5 (etcd member add) requires Phase 4
(binary sync). Phase 6 (service start) requires Phase 5 (etcd member added).
An AI agent must not add a shortcut that skips phases, and must not reorder
phases to "optimize" the flow.

failure_modes.title: Join token expires between Phase 1 and Phase 5 — ghost member left in etcd
failure_modes.summary:
A slow join (large binary download, slow network) may take long enough for the
join token to expire. If expiry is only checked at Phase 1 and not re-validated
at Phase 5, the gateway proceeds with an invalid token, signs a CSR for an
unauthenticated node, adds an etcd member, and then fails authentication checks
later — leaving a ghost etcd member and a mis-issued cert.
symptoms: join fails partway through with token expired error after long phase 4
etcd member list has a new entry that never became healthy
issued cert exists in pki/issued/ but the node never completed join
failure_modes.root_cause:
A slow join (large binary download, slow network) may take long enough for the
join token to expire. If expiry is only checked at Phase 1 and not re-validated
at Phase 5, the gateway proceeds with an invalid token, signs a CSR for an
unauthenticated node, adds an etcd member, and then fails authentication checks
later — leaving a ghost etcd member and a mis-issued cert.

failure_modes.title: CSR signed without verifying node identity or token — cert issued to unknown caller
failure_modes.summary:
If the CSR signing handler signs any CSR submitted to the join endpoint without
first validating the join token, the CSR subject matching the expected node
identity, and the CSR signature (proving key ownership), then any caller who
reaches the join endpoint can obtain a valid cluster CA-signed cert. This cert
can be used to pass mTLS checks for any cluster service.
symptoms: CA issues certs for nodes that never appeared in the approved join queue
Cert subject CN does not match the node hostname that was expected
CSR submitted without a join token succeeds
failure_modes.root_cause:
If the CSR signing handler signs any CSR submitted to the join endpoint without
first validating the join token, the CSR subject matching the expected node
identity, and the CSR signature (proving key ownership), then any caller who
reaches the join endpoint can obtain a valid cluster CA-signed cert. This cert
can be used to pass mTLS checks for any cluster service.

failure_modes.title: Envoy running with stale xDS snapshot after cert renewal or config change
failure_modes.summary:
When a TLS cert is renewed or an Envoy cluster/listener config changes, the xDS
server must push a new snapshot. If the push is missed (gateway restart, xDS crash,
or race between cert renewal and snapshot rebuild), Envoy continues serving with the
old config. The service appears healthy but is using an expired cert or routing to
a stale set of backends. Envoy does not self-detect snapshot staleness.
symptoms: Envoy admin /config_dump shows an older version string than xDS server
TLS handshake errors despite cert having been renewed in PKI
Traffic routed to a removed backend endpoint
xDS NACKs in Envoy log referencing old version ID
failure_modes.root_cause:
When a TLS cert is renewed or an Envoy cluster/listener config changes, the xDS
server must push a new snapshot. If the push is missed (gateway restart, xDS crash,
or race between cert renewal and snapshot rebuild), Envoy continues serving with the
old config. The service appears healthy but is using an expired cert or routing to
a stale set of backends. Envoy does not self-detect snapshot staleness.

failure_modes.title: CA cert missing at join time — CSR signing fails silently
failure_modes.summary:
If /var/lib/globular/pki/ca.key or ca.crt is absent when a node attempts to join,
the CSR signing step fails. Depending on error handling, the join may return a
generic error or silently issue an unsigned cert. The joining node installs a bad
cert and all subsequent mTLS connections fail. The root cause is usually a failed
prior bootstrap that left PKI in a partial state.
symptoms: join fails at Phase 3 or Phase 4 with certificate error
ls /var/lib/globular/pki/ shows ca.crt missing or zero-length
joining node service fails to start due to TLS handshake error
gateway log shows pki path not found or permission denied
failure_modes.root_cause:
If /var/lib/globular/pki/ca.key or ca.crt is absent when a node attempts to join,
the CSR signing step fails. Depending on error handling, the join may return a
generic error or silently issue an unsigned cert. The joining node installs a bad
cert and all subsequent mTLS connections fail. The root cause is usually a failed
prior bootstrap that left PKI in a partial state.

failure_modes.title: xDS serving without mTLS — Envoy accepts unsigned config from any caller
failure_modes.summary:
If GLOBULAR_XDS_INSECURE=1 is set in a production environment, the xDS server
disables mutual TLS. Any process that can reach the xDS port can push arbitrary
Envoy configuration — routing all traffic to an attacker-controlled backend,
replacing TLS secrets, or disabling auth entirely. This is a full cluster
compromise vector.
symptoms: xDS port is reachable without presenting a client cert
Envoy connects to xDS without mTLS (check Envoy admin /config_dump)
GLOBULAR_XDS_INSECURE=1 present in xDS service environment
failure_modes.root_cause:
If GLOBULAR_XDS_INSECURE=1 is set in a production environment, the xDS server
disables mutual TLS. Any process that can reach the xDS port can push arbitrary
Envoy configuration — routing all traffic to an attacker-controlled backend,
replacing TLS secrets, or disabling auth entirely. This is a full cluster
compromise vector.

failure_modes.title: Ghost etcd member from a failed prior join blocks new join attempts
failure_modes.summary:
If a node starts a join, reaches Phase 5 (etcd member add), but then fails before
completing Phase 6 (etcd start), the new member is registered in the etcd member
list but never comes online. Subsequent join attempts for the same node fail because
etcd rejects adding a member with the same peer URL or sees quorum already
reduced by the non-responsive ghost. The ghost must be explicitly removed.
symptoms: etcdctl member list shows a member with no client URLs or in unstarted state
new join attempt fails with cluster quorum error or member already exists
etcd cluster health shows unhealthy due to missing quorum from ghost
failure_modes.root_cause:
If a node starts a join, reaches Phase 5 (etcd member add), but then fails before
completing Phase 6 (etcd start), the new member is registered in the etcd member
list but never comes online. Subsequent join attempts for the same node fail because
etcd rejects adding a member with the same peer URL or sees quorum already
reduced by the non-responsive ghost. The ghost must be explicitly removed.
